# Product Requirements Document
# Generated by req-engine from raw input
# Feature: Workspace Module (full spec) + Comms Alerts page + Asset Module deep-link & multi-tab support
# Date: 2026-04-27
# Supersedes: prior Equipment Home Page PRD (2026-04-25). The Equipment Home Page changes here are
#             additive on top of the existing implementation.
# ---

## 1. Project Overview

SPM ONE is adding a full-featured Workspace Module — a per-user content management system for
dashboards — alongside an Alerts page in the Comms Module and deep-linking / multi-tab support
in the Assets Module. The Workspace becomes the single home for dashboard authoring,
organization, sharing, commenting, and publishing. Published dashboards continue to surface in
the Asset Module's Equipment Home Page, but the create/edit/share/manage lifecycle now lives
inside the Workspace. The Asset Module gains an "Open in new tab" full-screen viewer route so
the same dashboard can be monitored simultaneously across multiple browser tabs, with an
indicator on the Equipment Home Page when this is happening.

This is also the point at which the app introduces real Next.js routes (App Router) for new
surfaces — dashboards, full-screen viewers, and shared-link landings — instead of relying solely
on the in-store `currentView` switcher.

## 2. Goals & Success Criteria

1. Each user has a private Workspace listing every dashboard they created or that is shared
   with them, isolated from other users' workspaces.
2. Users can organize dashboards into a Canva-like nested folder hierarchy and filter them by
   equipment, status, creator, contributor, and date of last change.
3. Users can create, duplicate, move, rename, delete, share, and publish dashboards from the
   Workspace without leaving it.
4. Users can preview any of their created dashboards in a popup viewer with full-screen,
   commenting, and editing affordances; the editor is a Workspace-native editor that reuses the
   existing Widget Library catalog (no dashboard tabs at the bottom).
5. Users can share a dashboard with named users / emails or via a copyable link, choosing
   view / comment / edit permission, optionally sending a message and opting into "first view"
   notifications.
6. Users can see dashboards shared with them and request elevated permission (comment / edit);
   the sharer receives an Alert in the Comms Module.
7. The Comms Module surfaces an Alerts page listing all dashboard sharing notifications and
   permission requests, each with a "View" action that deep-links the user back to the
   relevant Workspace surface.
8. Clicking "Edit Dashboards" on an Equipment Home Page navigates the user to the Workspace
   Module pre-scoped to that equipment's dashboards.
9. The Equipment Home Page Dashboard Popup gains an "Open in new tab" action that opens the
   dashboard in a brand-new browser tab in fully full-screen mode and closes the popup in the
   originating tab; an indicator appears on the dashboard tab in the Equipment Home Page
   whenever that dashboard is open in any external tab. Each dashboard may be open in
   multiple tabs simultaneously.

## 3. Target Users

- **Primary user:** Integrity Engineer / Process Engineer — authors, organizes, shares, and
  monitors equipment dashboards from their personal Workspace.
- **Secondary user:** Other engineers in the same organization — receive shared dashboards,
  comment, request edit access, and view published dashboards in the Asset Module.

## 4. Functional Requirements

### 4.1 Workspace Module — Identity & Isolation

FR-01: The system shall scope every Workspace view to a single signed-in user; Workspace
       contents (folders, draft dashboards, drafts shared by others) shall not be visible to
       other users unless explicitly shared.
FR-02: The system shall represent the current user via a mock identity (no real auth) that is
       persistable across reloads using `localStorage`, so workspace ownership and sharing are
       deterministic across sessions.

### 4.2 Workspace Module — Dashboard Lifecycle States

FR-03: The system shall track each dashboard with a `lifecycleStatus` of either `created`
       (draft, only visible inside owner's and shared collaborators' Workspaces) or
       `published` (visible to authorised users in the Asset Module Equipment Home Page).
FR-04: The system shall allow the dashboard creator to publish a `created` dashboard and to
       unpublish a `published` dashboard back to `created`; both transitions shall be
       audit-logged in `ChangeLogEntry`.
FR-05: A published dashboard shall remain visible in its owner's Workspace (it is not
       removed when published).

### 4.3 Workspace Module — Dashboard Metadata

FR-06: Each dashboard shall record: equipment it belongs to, lifecycle status, creator
       (User), contributors (User[] — anyone with edit permission who saved a change),
       creation date, last-change date, last-change-by (User), folder it lives in (nullable).
FR-07: Each dashboard shall record a `thumbnailUrl` (mockable) used in card grids and search
       results.

### 4.4 Workspace Module — Folder Organization (Canva-like)

FR-08: The system shall provide a nested folder tree, owned by the user, with at minimum:
       create folder, rename folder, move folder (drag-and-drop or context menu), delete
       folder, and breadcrumb navigation.
FR-09: A folder shall be able to contain other folders (nesting depth shall not be limited
       artificially in the UI; minimum 5 levels supported).
FR-10: A dashboard shall belong to exactly zero or one folder at a time; "no folder" is the
       Workspace root.
FR-11: Deleting a folder shall require a confirmation dialog and shall present two choices:
       move contained items to root, or delete contained items (cascade).
FR-12: The Workspace shall display top-level virtual locations in the left navigation: "All
       Dashboards", "Shared with me", "Recent", "Trash" (or equivalent). User-created folders
       shall list below these.

### 4.5 Workspace Module — Browse, Search, Filter

FR-13: The Workspace main view shall render dashboards as cards in a responsive grid (and
       optionally a list view toggle).
FR-14: The system shall support text search over dashboard name, equipment name, creator name,
       and folder name with debounced input.
FR-15: The system shall support filtering by: equipment, lifecycle status (created /
       published), creator, contributor, and last-change date range. Active filters shall be
       displayed as removable chips.
FR-16: The system shall support sorting by: name, last-change date, creation date, equipment.
FR-17: Filter and sort state shall persist across navigation within the same session (Zustand);
       filter state shall not bleed across users.

### 4.6 Workspace Module — Dashboard CRUD Operations

FR-18: The system shall allow the user to create a new dashboard via "New Dashboard" button.
       The creation flow shall require the user to pick a target equipment and a name unique
       within that equipment.
FR-19: The system shall allow the user to duplicate any dashboard they own; the duplicate
       shall start in `created` state, copy the widget grid, prepend "Copy of" to the name,
       and place itself in the same folder as its source.
FR-20: The system shall allow the user to move a dashboard to any folder via drag-and-drop or
       a "Move to…" context menu action.
FR-21: The system shall allow the user to rename a dashboard inline from its card.
FR-22: The system shall allow the user to delete a dashboard they own with a confirmation
       dialog warning that all comments, shares, and notifications will be removed.

### 4.7 Workspace Module — Dashboard Popup Viewer

FR-23: Clicking a dashboard card shall open a Dashboard Popup overlay that renders the
       dashboard's widget grid, mirroring the Equipment Home Page popup pattern.
FR-24: The popup shall expose three top-level actions, each functioning independently of the
       others: (a) Expand to full screen (within the same tab), (b) Toggle Comments side
       panel, (c) Edit dashboard.
FR-25: The Edit and Comment actions shall always be available to the dashboard creator. For
       dashboards shared with the current user, Edit and Comment shall be enabled only if the
       sharer granted that permission level (see §4.10).
FR-26: The popup shall include an "Open in new tab" action that opens the dashboard in a new
       browser tab routed to the new full-screen Dashboard Viewer route (see §4.13) and
       simultaneously closes the popup in the originating tab.

### 4.8 Workspace Module — Comments

FR-27: The Comments side panel shall list comments in chronological order with author name,
       timestamp, and text; users with comment permission may post new comments.
FR-28: Comments shall be persisted in the Zustand mock store keyed by dashboard id.
FR-29: Comment events on dashboards owned by the current user shall not generate Alerts (only
       sharing-related events do, per §4.11 and §4.12).

### 4.9 Workspace Module — Editing (Workspace-native editor)

FR-30: The Edit action shall open a Workspace-native Dashboard Editor screen — distinct from
       the legacy Equipment Dashboard editor at `equipment-dashboard.tsx` — with no
       dashboard-tabs strip at the bottom.
FR-31: The Workspace editor shall reuse the existing Widget Library catalog and its
       drag-and-drop add interaction without modifying the Widget Library implementation.
FR-32: The Workspace editor shall support: drag widgets from library into grid, reposition
       widgets via drag, resize widgets, delete widgets, save changes, exit without saving.
FR-33: Saving a change as a non-creator with edit permission shall append the user to the
       dashboard's `contributors` set and update `lastChangeAt` and `lastChangeBy`.
FR-34: The editor shall be implemented as a NEW component (e.g.,
       `components/views/workspace/dashboard-editor.tsx`) that references but does not import
       the legacy editor's top-level component; shared atomic pieces (widget cards, library
       items, layout helpers) may be reused as long as the legacy editor remains untouched.

### 4.10 Workspace Module — Sharing with named users

FR-35: The system shall provide a Share dialog accessible from a dashboard card and from inside
       the Dashboard Popup.
FR-36: The Share dialog shall accept multiple recipients via a typeahead picker resolving
       organization users by name or email (mock directory of ≥ 8 users).
FR-37: For each share, the sharer shall pick exactly one permission level: `view`, `comment`,
       or `edit`.
FR-38: The Share dialog shall allow an optional plain-text message (≤ 500 characters) that is
       included in the recipient's Alert.
FR-39: The Share dialog shall offer a checkbox "Notify me when they first view this
       dashboard"; if checked, the sharer receives an Alert the first time the recipient opens
       the dashboard popup.
FR-40: The Share dialog shall list current shares for this dashboard with a per-row control to
       change permission level or revoke access.

### 4.11 Workspace Module — Sharing via link

FR-41: The Share dialog shall include a "Get link" tab that generates a unique copyable link
       per dashboard, with selectable permission level (view / comment / edit) and a Copy
       button.
FR-42: The system shall allow the owner to revoke or regenerate the link; revoked links shall
       refuse access (show "link no longer valid" state).
FR-43: A user opening a share link who is not signed in shall be routed to a stub
       sign-in/identity-pick screen (mock); after signing in, the system shall apply the link
       permission as if the dashboard had been shared with them by name.

### 4.12 Workspace Module — Receiving shared dashboards

FR-44: Dashboards shared with the current user shall appear in the "Shared with me" virtual
       location of the Workspace, never inside the user's own folders unless they explicitly
       move them.
FR-45: A shared dashboard card shall display the sharer's name, permission level, and shared
       date in addition to standard metadata.
FR-46: The Dashboard Popup viewer for a shared dashboard shall enable / disable the Comment
       and Edit actions based on the granted permission level.
FR-47: For a shared dashboard where the user lacks comment or edit permission, the popup shall
       expose a "Request access" control allowing them to request `comment` or `edit`. The
       request shall produce an Alert for the sharer (FR-50).

### 4.13 Asset Module — Equipment Home Page changes

FR-48: The "Edit Dashboards" button on the Equipment Home Page shall navigate the user to the
       Workspace Module, pre-filtered to dashboards belonging to the originating equipment;
       the equipment context shall persist via Zustand `preFilterEquipmentId` and a new
       `workspaceInitialEquipmentId` slice.
FR-49: The Equipment Home Page Dashboard Popup shall add an "Open in new tab" button that:
        (a) opens a new browser tab navigated to `/dashboards/[dashboardId]/full` (or
            equivalent App Router route),
        (b) closes the popup in the originating tab.
FR-50: The Dashboard Viewer route at `/dashboards/[dashboardId]/full` shall render the
       dashboard's widget grid in fully full-screen mode (no app sidebar, no header) and shall
       be read-only.
FR-51: The system shall maintain a per-dashboard "open elsewhere" indicator on the Equipment
       Home Page dashboard cards / tabs whenever the same dashboard is currently open in any
       other browser tab or window.
FR-52: The "open elsewhere" indicator shall update in real time using `BroadcastChannel` (or
       `localStorage`/`storage` event fallback for cross-tab signalling) and shall reflect the
       count of external open tabs.
FR-53: A dashboard may be open in multiple tabs simultaneously; the indicator shall remain
       visible while count ≥ 1.

### 4.14 Comms Module — Alerts page

FR-54: The Comms Module shall expose an Alerts page listing all alert items relevant to the
       current user, in reverse-chronological order.
FR-55: The Alerts page shall display, for each alert, a category icon, primary text
       (e.g., "Alice shared 'SMR Pigtail Integrity' with you (can edit)"), secondary text
       (sharer message excerpt or request reason), timestamp, read/unread state, and a
       "View" action.
FR-56: Alert categories supported in v1 shall include: `dashboard_shared_with_you`,
       `dashboard_first_view` (when sharer's "notify on first view" was enabled),
       `permission_request_received` (sharer side: someone asked for comment/edit),
       `permission_request_resolved` (recipient side: sharer granted/denied).
FR-57: Clicking the "View" action shall navigate the user to the Workspace Module:
        - `dashboard_shared_with_you` / `permission_request_resolved` → Workspace "Shared
          with me" with the dashboard card focused, opening its popup.
        - `dashboard_first_view` / `permission_request_received` → Workspace "All Dashboards"
          with the relevant dashboard card focused, opening its popup.
       The Alert shall be marked read on navigation.
FR-58: The Comms module rail icon shall display an unread-count badge that updates live.

### 4.15 Workspace Module Rail submenu (cleanup)

FR-59: The Workspace module rail submenu shall be cleaned up to provide a clean,
       efficient, professional set of entries that fit the new spec:
        - "All Dashboards"
        - "Shared with me"
        - "Recent"
        - "Trash"
       It shall replace, not duplicate, the current "Favorite" / "Share with me" entries.
       The system shall NOT introduce any additional sub-submenu beyond this.

### 4.16 App routing

FR-60: The app shall introduce App Router pages for at minimum:
        - `/workspace` — Workspace Module main view.
        - `/workspace/folder/[folderId]` — Workspace folder view.
        - `/workspace/shared` — Shared with me.
        - `/workspace/dashboard/[dashboardId]/edit` — Workspace-native editor.
        - `/dashboards/[dashboardId]/full` — Full-screen viewer (no chrome).
        - `/share/[linkToken]` — Share-link landing.
       The existing `currentView`-based router shall remain functional for legacy surfaces
       during a transitional period; new surfaces shall be URL-driven.

## 5. Non-Functional Requirements

NFR-01: Performance — Workspace main view shall render its skeleton in < 300 ms and switch to
        seeded mock content in < 1 500 ms with up to 200 mock dashboards across folders.
NFR-02: Performance — Folder tree drag-and-drop, dashboard card grid, and filter chips shall
        sustain 60 fps on a 1 440 × 900 viewport using virtualized rendering for ≥ 100 cards.
NFR-03: Accessibility — All Workspace surfaces shall meet WCAG 2.1 AA: focus rings, ARIA roles
        for tree (folder tree) and grid (dashboard grid), keyboard shortcuts (`/` focus
        search, `n` new dashboard, `Delete` delete focused card, `Enter` open popup).
NFR-04: Visual consistency — The Workspace and Comms surfaces shall reuse existing shadcn/ui
        primitives, Tailwind v4 utilities, and the project's two-layer sidebar architecture.
        No new design system shall be introduced.
NFR-05: Cross-tab signalling — Open-elsewhere detection shall work without backend support and
        shall degrade gracefully in browsers without `BroadcastChannel`.
NFR-06: Type safety — All new modules shall pass `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` with zero errors and
        zero new `any` usages in exported APIs.
NFR-07: Persistence — Workspace mock data (folders, dashboards, shares, comments,
        notifications, current-user identity) shall be persisted to `localStorage` so reloads
        preserve state, with a debug "Reset Workspace" action to seed fresh data.

## 6. Out of Scope

- Real authentication, role-based authorization, or backend-enforced sharing permissions.
- Real-time collaborative editing (operational transform / CRDT).
- Email or push notification delivery (Alerts are in-app only).
- Versioning / dashboard history / restore.
- Mobile-only viewports (< 1 024 px).
- Workspace dashboard publishing into Asset Module beyond updating its visibility flag and
  showing it on the Equipment Home Page (no new Asset Module surfaces).
- Renaming or expanding Workspace module rail beyond what FR-59 describes.

## 7. Constraints

- Tech stack is fixed: Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript 5.7, Tailwind v4,
  shadcn/ui, Zustand 5, Recharts, react-grid-layout.
- The existing legacy Equipment Dashboard editor (`components/views/equipment-dashboard.tsx`)
  must NOT be reused for the Workspace editor; build a new editor referencing it.
- The Widget Library implementation must NOT be modified (drop-in reuse only).
- Cross-tab signalling must be browser-native (no Service Workers / SSE / WebSockets).
- The Workspace module rail submenu must contain at most one level (no nested submenu).
- All navigation state that must survive reload (folder selection, current dashboard popup,
  full-screen tab) goes through real URLs, not Zustand.

## 8. Assumptions Made

- [ ] The mock current-user identity will be picked from a small dropdown in a debug panel
      (e.g., user-switcher) since there is no real auth — confirm this UX is acceptable.
- [ ] Folder nesting "Canva-like" means nested folders with drag-and-drop; depth ≥ 5 is
      sufficient for v1 — confirm.
- [ ] "Contributors" are derived (anyone with edit permission who saved at least once),
      not manually managed — confirm.
- [ ] Publishing a dashboard requires no review/approval; the creator publishes directly —
      confirm.
- [ ] Published dashboards remain editable by the creator inside the Workspace; edits are
      reflected immediately in the Asset Module — confirm.
- [ ] "Equipment a dashboard is created for" is set at creation time and cannot be changed
      later — confirm.
- [ ] Permission requests do not auto-grant; the sharer must approve via the Alert action
      menu — confirm.
- [ ] Each browser tab opening the full-screen viewer is detectable via a `BroadcastChannel`
      handshake; on close (`pagehide` / `beforeunload`) the tab broadcasts a leave message —
      confirm engineering approach.
- [ ] App Router migration only applies to new routes; existing `currentView` rendering
      remains for Home / Site / Plant / Equipment Home / Tools — confirm phased rollout.
- [ ] "Recent" virtual location in Workspace lists dashboards opened (popup or editor) by the
      current user, max 20, LRU — confirm.
- [ ] "Trash" holds soft-deleted dashboards for 30 days (mock) before permanent deletion —
      confirm whether Trash is in scope or should be cut.

## 9. Open Questions — RESOLVED 2026-04-27

- [x] Organization users are a **fixed mock list**; the Share dialog typeahead resolves only
      against this directory and there is no invite-new-user flow.
- [x] Share-link access is restricted to **organization users only**: a non-organization user
      who opens a link is blocked with a "not authorised" state. An organization user with a
      `comment` or `edit` link permission becomes a contributor on save (FR-33 applies).
- [x] On unpublish, existing `DashboardShare`s and `ShareLink`s **persist**. The Asset Module
      is conceptually a read-only "qualified dashboards" surface; publish/unpublish only
      toggles Asset Module visibility — sharing is independent.
- [x] The "open elsewhere" indicator is shown **only on the Equipment Home Page** (dashboard
      tabs and dashboard cards). Workspace dashboard cards do NOT show it.
- [x] The Workspace editor **locks to one editor at a time per dashboard, app-wide**. If a
      second tab attempts to open the editor for the same dashboard, entry is denied and a
      notification/toast `"This dashboard is being edited at the moment"` is displayed; the
      requester is offered to view read-only or wait. Lock is keyed by `dashboardId` and
      held in cross-tab state (`BroadcastChannel`/`localStorage`).
- [x] `Dashboard.thumbnailUrl` is populated via a **generated screenshot** of the rendered
      dashboard widget grid (captured on save). v1 may use a deterministic placeholder
      generator until a screenshot library is wired in.

## 10. Suggested Task Breakdown Hints

- **Domain & docs**: ontology entities (Folder, DashboardShare, ShareLink, DashboardComment,
  Notification, dashboard lifecycle fields, contributors), business rules, invariants;
  PROJECT.md update.
- **Mock identity & directory**: current-user store slice, mock organization users seed,
  user-switcher debug control.
- **Workspace store slice**: folders, dashboards, shares, links, comments, notifications,
  filters, sort, selection, recent list, trash; localStorage persistence layer.
- **Workspace data seeding**: replace `lib/workspace-data.ts` with a deterministic seed that
  produces folders, draft & published dashboards across multiple equipment, sample shares,
  sample comments, sample notifications.
- **App Router scaffolding**: introduce `/workspace`, `/workspace/folder/[id]`,
  `/workspace/shared`, `/workspace/dashboard/[id]/edit`, `/dashboards/[id]/full`,
  `/share/[token]` pages; integrate with existing `app/page.tsx` shell where appropriate.
- **Workspace shell UI**: top bar, left folder tree, main grid, breadcrumbs, view toggle.
- **Folder tree**: nested rendering, drag-and-drop, context menus, virtual locations.
- **Dashboard cards**: card component, hover actions, status badge, contributor avatars,
  thumbnail.
- **Search / filter / sort**: debounced search, filter chips, sort dropdown, persistence.
- **Dashboard popup viewer (Workspace)**: dialog, full-screen toggle, Comments panel, Edit
  button, Open-in-new-tab button.
- **Comments panel**: list, composer, permission-aware enabling.
- **Workspace-native dashboard editor**: new editor screen, Widget Library reuse, save flow,
  contributor stamping.
- **Share dialog (named users)**: typeahead, permission selector, message field, first-view
  notify, current-shares list.
- **Share dialog (link)**: link generation, permission selector, revoke / regenerate, copy.
- **Share-link landing route**: `/share/[token]` resolver, identity-pick stub.
- **Shared with me**: virtual location, shared metadata, request-access UI.
- **Comms Alerts page**: list, categories, "View" deep-link, mark-read, unread badge wiring.
- **Equipment Home Page integration**: rewire "Edit Dashboards" to Workspace pre-scoped;
  add "Open in new tab" to popup; dashboard tab indicator for cross-tab open state.
- **Cross-tab signalling utility**: `BroadcastChannel` wrapper with `storage`-event fallback
  exposing a hook (`useDashboardOpenTabs(dashboardId)`).
- **Full-screen viewer route**: chromeless layout, read-only widget grid, "leave" handshake.
- **Module rail submenu cleanup**: replace current Workspace panel entries with FR-59 set; no
  nested submenu.
- **Cleanup & migration**: remove obsolete Workspace stub, migrate any remaining
  `currentView === 'workspace'` callsites to URL-based navigation, verify legacy editor
  untouched.
- **Type-safety & lint sweep**: ensure tsc + lint clean.
